title: A549 cells, ZIKV infected, rep 1
characteristics_ch1.1: cell type: human lung adenocarcinoma cells
treatment_protocol_ch1: A549 cells were seeded a day before infection and incubated at 37oC overnight. The next day, cells were about 80% confluent, and one culture plate was trypsinized and counted to calculate the viral volume needed for a multiplicity of infection (moi) of 10. Cells were incubated with ZIKV or PBS for mock infection at 37oC for 1 hour, rocking every 15 minutes. Afterwards, 9 ml of media was added to cells and the ZIKV- and mock-infected cells were incubated at 37oC for 48 hours.
growth_protocol_ch1: Human lung adenocarcinoma cells (ATCC CCL-185) were maintained in Dulbecco’s minimal essential media (Gibco) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (Seradigm, Avantar), 10 mM nonessential amino acids (Gibco), 2 mM L-glutamine (Gibco) and grown at 37°C with 5% CO2.
extract_protocol_ch1: GENEWIZ protocol of RNA extraction with TRIzol.
extract_protocol_ch1.1: GENEWIZ library preparation protocol with polyA selected RNA using Illumina kit.
data_processing: The quality of each fastq file was checked using the FastQC program (version 0.11.9).
data_processing.1: Each fastq file underwent adapter trimming to improve quality using the program fastp (version 0.22.0).
data_processing.2: Trimmed fastq files were aligned to the human reference genome, hg38, using STAR (version 2.7.10a) with the --quantMode GeneCounts specified to obatin gene expression abundance files.
data_processing.4: Supplementary files format and content: Tab-delimited text files contain read count abundance files for each sample after adapter trimming.
contact_institute: University at Albany, State University of New York
